principles: Fork only when ownership boundaries are explicit.
Prefer two or three narrow parallel lanes over many vague agents.
Every fork must have its own acceptance criteria and output path.
If a task depends on a shared file with high churn, keep it in the parent lane.
do_not_fork_when: The task is mostly a single-file edit.
The acceptance criteria are still ambiguous.
The task is mostly review or arbitration rather than execution.
Two lanes would fight over the same root navigation or shared store file.
